programs.name: Medicaid Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and more; SLMB and QI cover Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries with limited income/assets (e.g., QMB: income ≤100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: Premium payments: Part B ($174.70/month in 2024); QMB also covers deductibles/copays.

programs.name: Home and Community Based Services Waiver for the Frail Elderly (HCBS FE Waiver)
programs.what_it_does: This statewide Medicaid waiver provides long-term services to prevent nursing home placement. Services include adult day care, respite care, homemaker services, and personal emergency response systems tailored to individual needs.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ at risk of nursing home admission with income up to 300% of Federal Benefit Rate ($2,982/month in 2026).
programs.benefit_value: Personalized services; does not cover room and board.

programs.name: Nevada PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: PACE offers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings. It includes adult day health care, transportation, and home care to support aging in place.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible and living in PACE service areas.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitated coverage for approved services.

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing food. In Nevada, it's administered as a federal program with state processing.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+; higher income disregard for elderly/disabled.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP assists with heating/cooling bills and crisis energy needs. Nevada administers it through local agencies for utility payments.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and sealing. It reduces energy costs for low-income homes.
programs.benefit_value: Free upgrades valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home.

programs.name: Nevada State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free counseling on Medicare options, appeals, and fraud prevention. Also known as Nevada Medicare Assistance Program (MAP).
programs.who_its_for: Nevada Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+ or under 65 disabled.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious meals delivered to homebound seniors. Often through Aging and Disability Services Division or local providers.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals.

programs.name: Respite Care Vouchers / Senior Respite Care
programs.what_it_does: Offers short-term relief for family caregivers through in-home or facility respite. Vouchers fund temporary caregiver breaks.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors aged 60+ or disabled adults.
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers up to $20-30/hour for respite services.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid training and employment in community service roles. Helps seniors gain skills and income.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work up to 20 hours/week.

programs.name: Nevada Advocates for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services for issues like Medicaid, guardianship, and consumer problems. Advocates for seniors' rights.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal aid consultations and representation.

programs.name: Nevada Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints. Protects rights in nursing homes and assisted living.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, group homes, assisted living aged 60+.

programs.name: Nevada's Senior RX Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides subsidies to help seniors cover prescription medication costs, including assistance with Medicare Part D expenses for eligible members and cost-sharing for those not eligible. Offers additional services like the Diabetic Sense program for medication compliance and vision benefits for qualifying members.
programs.who_its_for: Nevada residents aged 62 or older with household income not exceeding $25,477 (single) or $33,963 (married couples), who must enroll in an approved Medicare Part D plan if eligible.
programs.whats_unique: Nevada's state-funded pharmaceutical assistance program offering up to $5,100 annual subsidy, distinct from federal programs like Medicare Part D Extra Help.

programs.name: Community Options Program for the Elderly (COPE)
programs.what_it_does: Supplies non-medical services to support seniors remaining in their homes as an alternative to nursing homes. Assesses needs to provide assistance for those at risk of institutionalization.
programs.who_its_for: Nevada residents aged 65+ at risk of nursing home placement (meeting nursing home level of care) and financially eligible per current income guidelines.
programs.whats_unique: Nevada state program similar to but distinct from the HCBS FE Waiver, administered by Aging and Disability Services Division for home-based independence.
programs.benefit_value: Non-medical services tailored to prevent nursing home placement; value based on individual assessment[4]
